title: Client: text-selection → floating comment button → modal → submit
description:
FIRST STEP: 'git merge main --no-edit' in the worktree; verify /api/comment exists.

In assets/web (app.js, index.html, style.css): detect text selection that is fully within the MARKDOWN CONTENT AREA only (the page body container, not sidebar/frontmatter/TOC). On a non-empty selection, show a small floating 'Comment' button near the selection end. Clicking it opens a small modal: shows a short preview of the selected text, a textarea, Submit + Cancel. On Submit, POST /api/comment with {page (current page path), section (nearest preceding heading text), selected_text, comment}; on success show a brief confirmation and clear the selection/modal. Style with existing Clean-docs tokens (modal = surface bg, hairline border, single subtle shadow, accent submit button, focus rings). Esc/Cancel closes; clicking outside closes.

Test: select text in a rendered page, click Comment, type, Submit -> server records it (verify with kb.py comments list). Selecting text in the sidebar or TOC must NOT trigger the button.
notes: 2026-06-14 15:32 - [human] ANCHOR: compute the line number client-side — the page's raw markdown is already available from /api/page ({markdown}); locate the selected_text in it to get the 1-based start line (and end line if multi-line). Send page+line(+end_line)+section+selected_text+comment. Best-effort match (selected_text disambiguates); the agent gets path+line+text together.
acceptance_criteria: Selecting text in the content area shows a comment button → modal → submit POSTs to /api/comment with page+section+selected_text+comment; sidebar/TOC selections don't trigger it; styled per tokens
closed_reason: Completed: content-area-only selection → floating comment button → modal → submit; line/end_line computed from cached raw markdown; styled per Clean-docs tokens. (Browser-verified at epic end.)
